title: pterocarpus macrocarpus var. oblongus
intro: Pterocarpus macrocarpus var. oblongus, also known as Indian kino or bija, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. It is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its rich content of flavonoids and tannins. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
